commands.review_summary:
The branch broadens `nonSecretAuthMarkers` from bundled-only to plugin-owned manifests, drops the non-secret marker cache, skips disabled persisted index entries, updates docs, and adds focused tests.

PR surface: Source -1, Tests +103, Docs 0. Total +102 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: managed installs normally live under `configDir/extensions`, and the PR still scans that root after skipping disabled index entries. I did not run a local repro because this review was read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because this changes plugin credential-trust semantics, has a remaining active-owner bug, and still needs contributor-supplied real installed-plugin proof.

target.title: fix: honor plugin auth markers from manifests
target.body:
## Summary

- Honor plugin auth markers supplied by external manifests.
- Keep auth-marker behavior covered in the agents test shard.
- Preserve the fork-owned fix on fork `main` while offering this temporary branch for upstream review.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/model-auth-markers.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Model/auth marker handling now respects marker metadata from external plugin manifests instead of dropping that manifest-provided signal.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS at PR head `6100de63c389af2ba6eb2a9628b5d8340da7f756`; commands ran against the actual agents marker code.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/model-auth-markers.test.ts`; `git diff --check`.

Evidence after fix: Terminal capture from the local OpenClaw checkout:

```text
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/model-auth-markers.test.ts
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  9 passed (9)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 2.83s

$ git diff --check
exit status: 0
```

Observed result after fix: The manifest-auth-marker test shard exits 0 and confirms marker metadata is retained through the agents path.

What was not tested: A live third-party plugin install was not run from this machine; this PR validates the manifest marker behavior with the repository's focused agents coverage.

## Fork Context

This is a temporary upstream-review branch. The same accepted fix remains on `LDMB123/openclaw` fork `main`, per the fork cleanup plan.
